sprint_meta.notes: WK122 Round B (Agent 09, W1): single behavior-preserving fix to the HUD->GPU upload path in game/graphics/ursina_app_ui_overlay.py::_refresh_ui_overlay_texture. MEASURED: hud_texture_upload normally 0.4-10ms but periodically SPIKES to 41-47ms every few frames @ 20 heroes live. Root cause: dirty-region scan yields ONE spanning band dirty_min..dirty_max; since the HUD changes at BOTH the top (status/resource row) and the bottom (radar minimap unit dots move every frame), that band spans nearly the full screen height most frames, then runs the slow PNMImage round-trip (setRamImageAs(sub_bytes) -> store(sub_pnm) -> load_sub_image) which is ~44ms for a near-full band -- SLOWER than a single direct full upload. Render-only; no sim/AI; fingerprint/scan/small-band logic untouched.

wk122_w1.role: WAVE W1 - one behavior-preserving fix to the dirty-region branch of _refresh_ui_overlay_texture: when the dirty band is large, skip the slow PNMImage load_sub_image round-trip and do a direct full-texture upload instead. Same final on-screen pixels (full upload of raw_data vs sub-upload of a subset of the same bytes). Live HUD->GPU upload has no headless runtime coverage -> live FPS-spike verification DEFERRED to Sovereign end test per feedback_ursina_deferred_screenshots; shipped behind import/test/digest gates.

game/graphics/ursina_app_ui_overlay.py: Dirty-region branch (after `sub_h = dirty_max - dirty_min + 1`): added gate `if sub_h > int(sz[1] * 0.4): panda_tex.setRamImageAs(raw_data, "RGBA")` (direct full upload, the proven path) `else:` the EXISTING small-band load_sub_image try/except block UNCHANGED (now indented one level under else). Threshold = 40% of surface height per spec.
Small-band path (radar-only / thin deltas) preserved verbatim -- genuinely cheaper there. `owner._hud_prev_raw = raw_data` at the end of the branch left unchanged.

Diagnostic (one-time, silent unless KINGDOM_FPS_SLOWLOG set): after `sz = surf.get_size()` added a guarded one-shot print `[hud-size] surface=<sz> camera_aspect=<...>` gated by KINGDOM_FPS_SLOWLOG not in ('','0') and `owner._hud_size_logged` first-time flag.
NOT changed: _hud_quick_fingerprint, the top/bottom dirty-row scan, the small-band load_sub_image branch, the full-init/resize paths. Behavior-preserving; render-only.

sprint_meta.notes: WK123 Round B (Agent 09, W1): one behavior-preserving optimization to game/graphics/ursina_app_ui_overlay.py::_refresh_ui_overlay_texture. The WK122 single-band logic computed ONE spanning band dirty_min..dirty_max; with the HUD changing at BOTH the top status row (gold/wave) and the bottom-left radar (a dot per unit, ~80-monster swarm) the band spanned nearly full height and tripped the WK122 0.4*H cap into a full ~5.3MB setRamImageAs (~8-11ms). Replaced with a numpy MULTI-BAND approach: per-row dirty mask via np.any(cur!=prev,axis=1), contiguous run-finding via np.diff on the padded int mask, merge runs separated by <8 clean rows, then upload ONLY the merged runs (top run + bottom run, skipping the static middle) via the same load_sub_image mechanism. Full-upload fallback when total dirty rows > 0.5*H OR merged runs > 6, and on any per-band exception. Union of uploaded runs == exact changed rows -> pixel-identical to a full upload. Render-only; live FPS verification DEFERRED to Sovereign end test per feedback_ursina_deferred_screenshots.

wk123_w1.role: WAVE W1 - replace the single-band dirty-region upload in _refresh_ui_overlay_texture with a numpy multi-band approach that uploads only the actual contiguous changed row-runs (top status row + bottom radar) instead of a full-height spanning band. Behavior-preserving (identical final pixels); render-only. Live HUD->GPU upload has no headless runtime coverage -> live FPS-spike verification DEFERRED to Sovereign end test per feedback_ursina_deferred_screenshots; shipped behind import/test/digest gates.

game/graphics/ursina_app_ui_overlay.py: Replaced the WK122 single-band dirty logic (top-scan/bottom-scan -> dirty_min..dirty_max -> 0.4*H cap -> single load_sub_image) with a numpy multi-band path inside the same 'else:' branch (existing texture, same size).
not dirty.any() -> skip upload entirely (fingerprint false positive); still set _hud_prev_raw at end.
Contiguous run-finding: edges=np.diff(concat([0],dirty.int8,[0])); starts=where(edges==1); ends=where(edges==-1)-1. Merge runs separated by <8 clean rows (GAP=8) to avoid tiny fragmented uploads.
Path decision: total_dirty > 0.5*H OR len(merged) > 6 -> direct full setRamImageAs(raw_data) (cheaper than many sub-uploads). Else: per merged run [r0,r1] call local helper _upload_band(r0,r1).

_upload_band(r0,r1): factored from the WK122 single-band sub-upload verbatim -- builds temp PandaTexture of just those rows, store()->PNMImage, load_sub_image at panda_y=H-r0-sub_h (GPU y=0 at bottom; pygame top-down RAM). Same mechanism as before, per run.
On ANY exception during band upload -> fall back to full setRamImageAs(raw_data) and stop (never leave a partial frame). Outer try also falls back to full upload on numpy/setup failure.

PRESERVED unchanged: _hud_quick_fingerprint + quick-sig gate, size-change/full-init upload paths, GPU Y-inversion setup (texture_scale=(1,-1), texture_offset=(0,1)), and the 'owner._hud_prev_raw = raw_data' assignment at end of branch.

evidence.pixel_identity_check: Standalone numpy harness on a 1080x1920 top(rows0-18)+bottom(rows1040-1075) dirty pattern: runs found [(0,18),(1040,1075)], not merged (gap>>8), union of uploaded rows == exact dirty rows (np.array_equal True). 55 dirty rows < 540 (0.5*H) and 2 runs <= 6 -> multi-band path taken (was full ~5.3MB upload under WK122). Small-gap(<8) runs merge correctly; merged-band re-uploads clean gap rows with identical bytes -> pixels still identical. >6 fragmented runs -> full-upload fallback.

wk123_w1.notes: Behavior-preserving render-only. The union of uploaded row-runs is exactly the set of changed rows, so on-screen texture == full upload, just cheaper. Did NOT commit/add/push per prompt.

sprint_meta.notes: White-squares unit fix (GPU instancing default path). Units rendered as solid WHITE squares: the instanced fragment shader sampled its atlas via the RESERVED Panda3D auto-input p3d_Texture0, which Panda auto-binds to texture stage 0 (empty on the raw instanced GeomNode -> default WHITE), overriding the manual set_shader_input. Renamed the atlas sampler to a CUSTOM name unitAtlas in both the GLSL and the two renderer binds so the manual bind takes effect. Positions were already correct (they come from the custom-named instanceData samplerBuffer), confirming the mechanism was binding-not-data.

white_squares_fix_w1.role: Agent 09 ArtDirector. Fix confirmed visual bug: instanced units render as solid white squares (no sprite texture). Render-only; no sim/gameplay change.

game/graphics/instanced_unit_shader.py: L49: uniform sampler2D p3d_Texture0; -> uniform sampler2D unitAtlas;
L54: vec4 texColor = texture(p3d_Texture0, uvs); -> texture(unitAtlas, uvs);
Vertex shader, discard (a<0.08), UV math, and compile(shader_includes=False) call all unchanged.
game/graphics/instanced_unit_renderer.py: L232 (_ensure_initialized, outside geom): set_shader_input("p3d_Texture0", panda_atlas) -> set_shader_input("unitAtlas", panda_atlas).
L239 (_ensure_initialized, inside geom): same rename to "unitAtlas".
Shadow shader / shadow geom NOT touched (it does not sample the atlas).

white_squares_fix_w1.root_cause: p3d_Texture0 is a reserved Panda3D auto-input bound to node texture stage 0 (empty -> default white, opaque alpha=1 passes the a<0.08 discard), which overrode the manual set_shader_input. instanceData (custom name) bound fine, so positions were correct -> proved binding-not-data.
white_squares_fix_w1.grep_check: grep p3d_Texture0 in both instanced unit files = no matches after rename. Unrelated p3d_Texture0 in terrain_fog_shader.py / ursina_sprite_unlit_shader.py / terrain_geomipterrain.py left untouched.

invisible_units_atlas_stage0_w2.role: Agent 09 ArtDirector. Fix instanced units rendering INVISIBLE (transparent) after the w1 white-squares fix. Render-only; no sim/gameplay change.

game/graphics/instanced_unit_shader.py: fragment: uniform sampler2D unitAtlas; -> uniform sampler2D p3d_Texture0; (reverted to the reserved Panda auto-input).
fragment: vec4 texColor = texture(unitAtlas, uvs); -> texture(p3d_Texture0, uvs);
Vertex shader, discard (a<0.08), UV math, and compile(shader_includes=False) call all unchanged.
game/graphics/instanced_unit_renderer.py: _ensure_initialized (outside geom): deleted self._geom_node_outside.set_shader_input("unitAtlas", panda_atlas) and replaced with self._geom_node_outside.set_texture(panda_atlas, 1) so Panda auto-binds stage-0 tex to the shader p3d_Texture0 (mirrors legacy Entity.texture path). Added explanatory comment.
_ensure_initialized (inside geom): deleted self._geom_node_inside.set_shader_input("unitAtlas", panda_atlas) and replaced with self._geom_node_inside.set_texture(panda_atlas, 1).
KEPT both set_shader_input("instanceData", ...) binds (samplerBuffer position source — binds fine, units were already positioned correctly).
Shadow shader / shadow geom NOT touched (it does not sample the atlas).

invisible_units_atlas_stage0_w2.root_cause: w1 renamed the sampler to a custom name unitAtlas + set_shader_input("unitAtlas", atlas). For a sampler2D, set_shader_input did not bind here (texture-unit clash with the instanceData samplerBuffer / bind did not take) -> sampler read unbound -> texture() returned transparent -> units INVISIBLE. The reserved p3d_Texture0 auto-input + NodePath.set_texture(atlas, priority=1) on stage 0 is exactly how the working legacy Entity.texture billboard path binds, so it is the proven mechanism.

invisible_units_vflip_atlassource_w3.role: Agent 09 ArtDirector. Fix instanced units STILL rendering INVISIBLE after w2. Root cause found by comparing to the working legacy billboard path. Render-only; no sim/gameplay change.

game/graphics/instanced_unit_shader.py: VERTEX shader: replaced `uvs = uvRegion.xy + p3d_MultiTexCoord0 * uvRegion.zw;` with a V-FLIPPED form mirroring the legacy texture_offset `1.0 - v - vh`:
float _v0 = 1.0 - uvRegion.y - uvRegion.w;   // V-flip: pygame top-down -> GL bottom-up (matches legacy texture_offset)

U component formula unchanged (negative uvRegion.z width still handles the horizontal facing-flip). Fragment shader (p3d_Texture0 sampler), discard (a<0.08), and compile(shader_includes=False) all UNCHANGED.
game/graphics/instanced_unit_renderer.py: _ensure_initialized: atlas-source line changed from `self._atlas_tex = pygame_surface_to_ursina_texture(atlas_surf, cache_key="unit_instancing_atlas_v1")` to `self._atlas_tex = UnitAtlasBuilder.get().get_ursina_texture()` (the SAME atlas Texture object as the legacy `_sync_unit_atlas_billboard`, built via TerrainTextureBridge).
Removed the now-unused `from game.graphics.ursina_texture_bridge import pygame_surface_to_ursina_texture` import (no other reference in the module).

KEPT `panda_atlas = self._atlas_tex._texture`, both `set_texture(panda_atlas, 1)` stage binds, the FT_nearest mag/min filter calls on panda_atlas, and both `set_shader_input("instanceData", ...)` binds.
Shadow shader / shadow geom NOT touched (it does not sample the atlas).

invisible_units_vflip_atlassource_w3.root_cause: Two mismatches vs the proven legacy billboard path. (1) The legacy texture_offset V-flips the atlas V coord (`1.0 - v - vh`) for the pygame top-down -> GL bottom-up convention; the instanced shader used uvRegion.y DIRECTLY, sampling the mirrored (empty/transparent) V band -> texColor.a ~ 0 -> the `if (texColor.a < 0.08) discard;` made every unit invisible. (2) The instanced path built its atlas via a different converter (pygame_surface_to_ursina_texture) than the legacy `UnitAtlasBuilder.get_ursina_texture()`, risking divergent V handling. Both now match the legacy setup; the atlas surface itself was confirmed good (2048x2048, warrior idle0 at uv (0,0,0.0234,0.0234)).

r1.role: Agent 09 ArtDirector (Wave 1). Two precise, behavior-preserving fixes from the PM-diagnosed tickets: T1 (overlay render-bin ordering) and T5 (worker-sprite missing-action fallback). No VFX/vfx.py work this round.

game/graphics/ursina_unit_overlays.py: configure_ks_overlay: fixed the SELF-CANCELLING bin. OLD order: set_bin('fixed',60) THEN always_on_top=True -- Ursina's always_on_top setter internally calls set_bin('fixed',0)+depth-off, clobbering the high bin back to sort 0, so labels ended at fixed,0 and drew UNDER buildings (fixed,1). NEW order: billboard=True; always_on_top=True FIRST (let its setter reset bin->0 + depth off); then re-assert set_depth_test(False)+set_depth_write(False); then set_bin('fixed',110) LAST so nothing clobbers it. 110 > buildings(fixed,1) AND instanced units(fixed,100).
Removed the dead `ent.render_queue = 2` line (no-op for Text -- Text has no .model; setter only acts on self.model).

Kept unchanged: the early-return _ks_overlay_cfg guard, the z>=0 -> z=-0.02 nudge, the trailing ent._ks_overlay_cfg=True. Updated the docstring to record the root cause + ordering rule. Shared helper -> HP bars, name labels, hero $N/Zzz, tax-collector gold, building tax-gold $N all now correctly draw on top (net-positive).
game/graphics/worker_sprites.py: clips_for: two-pass build. Pass 1 builds clips ONLY from actions with real PNG frames; actions with no art are collected into missing_actions (was: immediately filled from _procedural_frames -> the letter-on-circle 'P' placeholder). Pass 2: if missing_actions, pick base_clip = clips['idle'] (or first available clip); if base_clip is None (unit has NO real art at all) keep the legacy procedural fallback; else fill each missing action by reusing list(base_clip.frames) (this unit's real idle frames). Clips are cached -> static, not per-frame. Fixes peasant_builder hurt/dead (idle/walk/work exist, no hurt/dead folders) to show the real green builder art instead of the P-circle. Regular peasant has its own hurt/dead PNGs so it loads them in pass 1 and is unaffected.
tests/test_wk124_overlay_bin.py: NEW. Layer 1 (no GPU, never skips): a _FakeAlwaysOnTopNode whose always_on_top setter clobbers the bin to ('fixed',0) like Ursina's; asserts configure_ks_overlay leaves the FINAL set_bin == ('fixed',110), plus depth off and idempotent-guard no-op on 2nd call. Layer 2 (real offscreen Ursina, skips cleanly if Panda3D/Ursina can't init): builds a real Text, asserts getBinName()=='fixed' and getBinDrawOrder()==110.
tests/test_wk124_peasant_builder_clips.p: NEW. Asserts clips_for('peasant_builder')['hurt'].frames and ['dead'].frames are byte-identical to ['idle'].frames (real fallback, not the P-circle); a direct guard that the builder hurt frames are NOT equal to _procedural_frames('peasant_builder','hurt',...); and that regular clips_for('peasant')['hurt'].frames != ['idle'].frames (keeps its own hurt art). Clears WorkerSpriteLibrary._cache before/after each test; inits headless pygame.

r1.why: T1: tax-gold $N (and other world overlays) were occluded by tall/near buildings because the high render bin was being reset to 0 by always_on_top -- a self-cancelling sequence (continuation of WK122-BUG-A2). T5: builder peasants flashed the 'circle-with-a-P' placeholder when hit/killed because peasant_builder ships no hurt/dead PNGs and the old code synthesized those from the procedural placeholder.

wk124_tests.tail: 6 passed, 1 warning in 2.17s (incl. the real offscreen-Ursina getBinDrawOrder()==110 assertion -- NOT skipped on this GPU box)

wk67_digest_confidence.tail: 10 passed -- render-only edits are inert; AI-decision digest byte-identical (not a required gate for T1/T5 but verified for confidence)

evidence.manual_probe: Confirmed offscreen Ursina on this box: buggy order (set_bin then always_on_top) -> getBinDrawOrder()==0 (the occlusion bug); fixed order -> ==110. Confirmed on disk: peasant_builder has only idle/walk/work folders (no hurt/dead); peasant has hurt(4)/dead(1).

assumptions: T5 base-clip choice is idle when present (per ticket); if idle were absent it uses the first available real clip, and only true zero-art units keep the procedural placeholder. peasant_builder has idle, so hurt/dead reuse idle.

wave2.role: Agent 09 ArtDirector (Wave 2). T3c (wizard magic projectile billboard) + T4c (cleric heal VFX, visible in pygame + Ursina), consuming Agent 05's landed contracts (kind:'magic' on RANGED_PROJECTILE + new HERO_HEAL event).

contracts_verified_from_code.projectile_kind_field: game/systems/combat.py:199 emits event field 'projectile_kind' (value from (spec or {}).get('kind','arrow')); color via 'color' (spec 'color'); size via 'size_px'. vfx.py:_emit_event already reads event.get('projectile_kind','arrow') + 'color' + 'size_px' and passes them to _spawn_projectile. Wizard: Hero.get_ranged_spec() (game/entities/hero.py:575-580) returns {'kind':'magic','color':WIZARD_SPELL_COLOR=(170,90,230),'size_px':WIZARD_SPELL_SIZE_PX=4}; every non-wizard returns None (ranger arrow unchanged).

game/graphics/vfx.py: ProjectileVFX dataclass: added field `kind: str = 'arrow'` (additive default; 'arrow'|'magic'|'heal'). Picks the 3D billboard in the Ursina sync.

_emit_event: added a `et == 'hero_heal'` case BEFORE the hero_attack branch (after the x/y None-guard): calls _spawn_heal(x,y) for the pygame green/gold particle burst AND, when from_x/from_y present, _spawn_projectile(from_x,from_y,x,y,'heal',None,3) so a green heal bolt is also visible via the Ursina billboard path. Returns after.

_spawn_projectile: made kind-aware. For kind=='magic' honors the spec color (default (170,90,230) WIZARD_SPELL_COLOR) + lilac highlight, size clamped 2..4 (default 4). For kind=='heal' green core (default (90,220,120)) + pale-gold highlight, size 2..4 (default 3). For arrow (default/unknown) keeps the EXACT prior behavior: forced SaddleBrown (139,69,19) + near-white tip + 1->2 px upgrade clamp. Stores kind on the ProjectileVFX. Updated docstring.

_spawn_heal(x,y): NEW. Green/gold particle burst modeled on _spawn_big but lighter + biased upward (vy-45 to rise). Deterministic position-seeded random.Random((int(x)<<16)^int(y)^0x4EA1) -- no wall-clock/global randomness. 14 motes, ~70% soft green / ~30% gold, 2-3px, life 0.40-0.65s.

Added cached billboard surfaces: _make_orb_surface(core_rgb,halo_rgb) builds a 32x32 RGBA glowing orb (3 translucent halo rings -> solid core -> bright highlight). get_magic_billboard_surface() (purple core 170,90,230 / halo 200,150,255) and get_heal_billboard_surface() (green core 90,220,120 / halo 170,255,190), each module-cached in _MAGIC_BILLBOARD_CACHE / _HEAL_BILLBOARD_CACHE -> generated ONCE (FPS guardrail: no per-frame surface regeneration). Mirrors existing get_projectile_billboard_surface().
game/graphics/ursina_misc_props_sync.py: Import: added get_magic_billboard_surface + get_heal_billboard_surface alongside get_projectile_billboard_surface.

sync_snapshot_projectiles: cache the magic + heal textures ONCE on the renderer via getattr(r,'_magic_tex',None)/getattr(r,'_heal_tex',None) (renderer __init__ only declares _projectile_tex; getattr fallback safe since I cannot edit ursina_renderer.py this lane) using TerrainTextureBridge.surface_to_texture with cache_keys ('ursina','projectile_magic_billboard_v1') / ('ursina','projectile_heal_billboard_v1').

Per projectile: pick tex by getattr(proj,'kind','arrow') -> magic=_magic_tex, heal=_heal_tex, else arrow _projectile_tex. Threaded the chosen `tex` into BOTH get_or_create_entity(texture=tex) and sync_billboard_entity(tex=tex) (was hardcoded ptex). Arrow path is byte-identical for kind=='arrow'. No per-frame surface/texture regeneration (FPS guardrail).

Adding `kind` with a default is a purely additive extension; the test passed unchanged (in the 52-test subset). The other vfx_projectiles consumer, game/graphics/instanced_unit_renderer.py:575, reads only proj.x/proj.y -- not affected by the new field.

evidence.behavior_sanity: Standalone probe: ranged_projectile kind:'magic' color:(170,90,230) size:4 -> ProjectileVFX.kind=='magic', color preserved (170,90,230); kind:'arrow' -> color forced (139,69,19) (brown preserved); hero_heal event -> 1 heal-bolt projectile (kind=='heal', color (90,220,120)) + 14 deterministic particles; two identical heal events produce identical particle layouts (deterministic). get_magic/heal_billboard_surface() are cached (same object on 2nd call), 32x32.

assumptions: Renderer __init__ does not pre-declare _magic_tex/_heal_tex (only _projectile_tex at ursina_renderer.py:334). I cannot edit ursina_renderer.py this lane, so I used getattr(r,'_magic_tex',None)/getattr(r,'_heal_tex',None) lazy-init on first projectile sync -- consistent with the existing _projectile_tex None-guard pattern, zero per-frame cost after first use.

Magic/heal billboards are MVP procedural glowing orbs (purple/green) per the ticket -- the Wizard-Attack01_Effect / Priest-Heal_Effect sprite-sheet slicing is an explicit STRETCH and was NOT done (no asset files added; tools/assets_manifest.json + validate_assets NOT touched).
STRETCH '+N green flash on the healed HP bar' was SKIPPED per the ticket's 'skip if it risks the HP-bar code' guidance (HP-bar code is outside this lane).
_spawn_projectile applies particle gravity in update() (vy*0.90 + 35*dt); heal motes use vy-45 so they net-rise briefly then settle -- intended.

r2_t8_quest_giver_render_fix.ticket: WK133 T8 fix round: quest-giver NPC + '!' marker not rendering in Ursina (PM live-capture FAIL)
r2_t8_quest_giver_render_fix.root_cause: RenderSnapshot (game/sim/snapshot.py) has no quest-giver field and SimEngine.build_snapshot never exports self.quest_givers (the r1 handoff to Agent 05/03 was never executed). snapshot_quest_giver_states() is tolerated-absent by design, so it ALWAYS returned () and sync_snapshot_quest_givers — correctly hooked in BOTH renderer branches since r1 — iterated nothing. The QuestGiver existed sim-side with is_open=True; the data simply never crossed the render boundary.
r2_t8_quest_giver_render_fix.hook_point: game/graphics/ursina_app_frame.py run_frame(): immediately after snapshot = owner.engine.build_snapshot(), call ursina_unit_sync.attach_quest_giver_states(snapshot, owner.engine). This file is graphics-lane and is the render-boundary owner of the snapshot handoff, so the export lives render-side (sim lane untouched, WK67 digest byte-safe — the headless digest never builds render snapshots nor runs ursina_app_frame). The helper value-copies each live giver into a frozen QuestGiverRenderState NamedTuple (giver_id/x/y/is_open/is_alive — L1 no-live-object rule preserved) and object.__setattr__s the tuple as snapshot.quest_givers — the exact fallback field snapshot_quest_giver_states already reads; the sim's own quest_giver_dtos field still wins if a later wave adds it. No-op (1 getattr) when no givers exist.
what_i_changed: game/graphics/ursina_unit_sync.py: new QuestGiverRenderState NamedTuple + attach_quest_giver_states(snapshot, engine) (render-boundary export, see hook_point).

Marker call (sync_quest_giver_marker(ent, qg.is_open)) + quest_giver:<giver_id> keying + underground gate + _OVERLAY_CHILD_ATTRS teardown all unchanged.
game/graphics/ursina_app_frame.py: 2-line hook after build_snapshot (lazy import + attach call).

sprint_meta.notes: URGENT fix round: full-suite Windows access violation (0xC0000005) killing the entire pytest process ~44% in; faulthandler pointed at font_cache.render_text_cached via quest_giver_renderer in test_wk126_quest_marker_overlay. Order-dependent: an earlier test pygame.quit() left module-level cached Font objects dangling; next native render = uncatchable AV.

wk126_urgent_w1.role: Make font_cache teardown-safe (generation counter + quit hooks) and make the wk126 pygame marker tests self-initializing; reproduce first, prove after.
wk126_urgent_w1.reproducer: python -m pytest tests/test_renderer_snapshot_contract.py tests/test_wk126_quest_marker_overlay.py -q -> access violation at font_cache.py:48 (exit -1073741819) BEFORE the fix; 18 passed AFTER. test_renderer_snapshot_contract.py drives a full renderer pass (populates _FONT_CACHE) then pygame.quit() with no cache flush; the marker test re-inits pygame and hits a stale Font (use-after-free in SDL_ttf).

game/graphics/font_cache.py: Added _FONT_GENERATION init-cycle counter; _FONT_CACHE now maps size -> (generation, Font).

get_font returns a cached Font only if its generation matches (one int compare on the healthy hot path - per-frame HUD, FPS guardrails); on miss/stale it re-inits pygame.font if needed and rebuilds the entry.

_install_quit_hooks() (idempotent, marker attr on pygame) wraps pygame.quit AND pygame.font.quit to bump the generation and flush _FONT_CACHE/_TEXT_CACHE/_TEXT_SHADOW_CACHE before native teardown - protects EVERY font_cache caller, not just quest_giver_renderer. pygame.quit tears font down at C level without routing through pygame.font.quit, hence both wraps.
tests/test_wk126_quest_marker_overlay.py: pygame_surface fixture now explicitly self-initializes (pygame.init() + pygame.font.init()) with a docstring pinning the order-independence requirement.

gates: tests/test_wk66_render_boundary.py (direct _FONT_CACHE dict consumer, only .clear()s - shape-compatible): 7 passed 1 skipped
hook sanity: font.quit and pygame.quit both bump generation + flush caches; render after quit/re-init cycle works (pygame.quit may bump twice via font autoquit - harmless over-invalidation)
